text.title: AI Over-Reliance Self-Audit Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every AI Over-Reliance Self-Audit application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: List every module touched by AI in last 90 days. Rate comprehension 1-5 (5 = rewrite from memory; 1 = read it once).
rule.rationale: Cannot fix what you cannot measure; scoring forces honesty.

rule.statement: For every architectural decision in last 90 days, record: was this MY decision, or did I accept the model's suggestion without alternatives? Target ≤30% rubber-stamp.
rule.rationale: Decision ownership is the moat; rubber-stamping erodes ownership silently.

rule.statement: Pick 3 lowest-comprehension modules. Schedule 'understand or rewrite' time: read line-by-line, add comments in own words, run tests. If can't understand after 1h, rewrite.
rule.rationale: Bounded recovery; 3 modules × quarter is sustainable.

rule.statement: Refuse to add features to modules scoring ≤2 until recovered. New features in foggy code compound debt 2-3×.
Run audit every 90 days. Skip-quarters are first sign of denial. Calendar-block; do not 'find time'.
rule.rationale: Habit literature; scheduled review beats availability-driven review.
